scope: whole library — public entry API: model-building, FEMData/resolution, OpenSees bridge, Results, cuts/sweeps/drift, ground motions, viewers (entry), viz, FEM-theory helpers

methods.label: AlongBeam
methods.summary: Tangent-derived orientation whose e3 follows the nearest reference-PG line tangent; passed as orientation= to a geomTransf.

flow.action: hold reference_pg name; bridge calls bind_fem(fem) before fan-out to cache reference segments
flow.passes: AlongBeam orientation field + reference PG name

flow.action: bind_fem reads fem.elements.get(pg)+fem.nodes.coords; triad_at projects element point onto nearest segment for per-element vecxz

methods.inputs: reference_pg:str (must contain 2-node line elements on the FEM snapshot)

methods.label: BuiltModel
methods.signature: BuiltModel(primitives, tag_for, ndm, ndf, fem, fix_records, mass_records)
methods.summary: Immutable frozen snapshot of declared primitives + tag map + FEMData that emitters consume.

flow.action: hold frozen primitive tuple + id→tag map + FEMData ref

methods.inputs: primitives:tuple[Primitive,...], tag_for:dict[int,int], ndm:int, ndf:int, fem:FEMData, fix_records, mass_records

methods.label: emit
methods.summary: Drive an emitter over the whole model in topological order (materials/sections→transforms→elements→patterns); returns 0.

flow.action: seed TagAllocator + install base tag resolver on emitter

emit model() then every fem.nodes id/coords as emitter.node(); per-node ndf flows via fem.nodes.ndf_for(tag) wrapped in try/except LookupError (override-only — sentinel slots elide -ndf and fall back to the envelope per ADR 0033)
flow.passes: node tag + xyz floats + per-node ndf|None

flow.action: topological_order primitives, _emit each material/section/integration/transform, fan elements over PG, fix/mass/broker-loads, patterns/recorders
flow.passes: per-primitive tag ints + per-element node tuples

methods.label: Cartesian
methods.summary: Constant Cartesian orientation triad (default Z-up) passed as orientation= to a geomTransf.

flow.action: compute orthonormal triad from reference_axis at construction
transform.orientation triad_at() consumed by emit_transform_specs to derive vecxz

methods.outputs: Cartesian orientation (triad_at(p) -> (e1,e2,e3)); also the bridge default_orientation

methods.label: Cylindrical
methods.summary: Cylindrical orientation triad about an axis of revolution passed as orientation= to a geomTransf.

flow.action: store origin+unit axis; triad_at computes radial/circumferential/axial per point
emit_transform_specs calls triad_at(element-point) to derive per-element vecxz

methods.signature: @dataclass(frozen=True) GaussSlab(component, values: ndarray (T,sum_GP), element_index: ndarray (sum_GP,), natural_coords: ndarray (sum_GP,dim), local_axes_quaternion: Optional[ndarray] (E,4), time: ndarray (T,)) ; global_coords(fem) -> ndarray (sum_GP,3)

methods.inputs: (constructed by the reader); global_coords takes a bound fem
methods.outputs: frozen GaussSlab dataclass + global_coords(fem) world-coord ndarray

methods.label: difference
methods.summary: Set difference of two compatible GeometryChains (self minus other).

flow.action: _compatible(other); drop=set(other._items); filter self._items not in drop

methods.inputs: other: another GeometryChain bound to the same engine
methods.outputs: a new GeometryChain = self − other; operator a - b

methods.label: in_box
methods.summary: Refine the chain to entities whose BRep bbox lies in [lo,hi]; inclusive= forbidden.

flow.action: reject any kw (inclusive= inexpressible for entity family); _spatial_box
getEntitiesInBoundingBox per distinct dim; intersect with chain atoms preserving order

methods.inputs: lo,hi: lower/upper 3-corners; NO keywords (TypeError if inclusive= or any kw passed — R3)
methods.outputs: a refined GeometryChain (Gmsh BRep bbox-containment, intersected with current atoms)

methods.label: in_sphere
methods.summary: Refine to entities whose bbox centre is within radius of center.
methods.outputs: a refined GeometryChain (closed ball on entity bbox centre — coarse proxy)

methods.label: intersect
methods.summary: Intersection of two compatible GeometryChains.
methods.inputs: other: another GeometryChain bound to the same engine
methods.outputs: a new GeometryChain = self ∩ other; operator a & b

methods.label: nearest_to
methods.summary: Refine to the count entities whose bbox centre is nearest a point.

flow.action: _nearest: _coords_of = bbox centres; sort by squared distance (index tie-break); take count

methods.outputs: a refined GeometryChain of the count nearest entities (by bbox-centre distance)

methods.label: on_plane
methods.summary: Refine to entities lying entirely on a plane (all 8 bbox corners within tol).

flow.action: _spatial_plane: build Plane(normal,anchor); per atom getBoundingBox; keep if all 8 |signed_dist|<=tol

methods.outputs: a refined GeometryChain (legacy on= 'on' test through the unified surface)

methods.label: result
methods.summary: Materialise the chain into the legacy Selection terminal (unchanged .to_label/.to_physical/.tags).

flow.action: return legacy Selection wired exactly like queries.select(...) output

methods.outputs: a legacy Selection (so .to_label/.to_physical/.tags/.select(on=) all work through the byte-unchanged terminal)

methods.label: symmetric_difference
methods.summary: Symmetric difference of two compatible GeometryChains.

flow.action: _compatible(other); only = set(self)^set(other); preserve order across self+other

methods.inputs: other: another GeometryChain bound to the same engine
methods.outputs: a new GeometryChain = self △ other; operator a ^ b

methods.label: union
methods.summary: Union of two compatible GeometryChains (same type + engine), order-preserving dedup.
methods.inputs: other: another GeometryChain bound to the same engine
methods.outputs: a new GeometryChain = self ∪ other; operator a | b

methods.label: ResultChain.difference / -
methods.summary: Set-algebra verb (inherited): keep atoms in self not in other. Same _compatible gate as union. Returns a new ResultChain.

flow.action: _wrap(a for a in self if a not in set(other))

methods.inputs: other (a ResultChain from the same composite/level)

methods.label: ResultChain.get
methods.signature: get(*, component: str, time=None, stage=None, **extra) -> NodeSlab | ElementSlab | GaussSlab | FiberSlab | LayerSlab | LineStationSlab | SpringSlab
methods.summary: Chain terminal: materialise the slab for the chain's narrowed ids. Delegates verbatim to the spawning composite's existing .get(ids=list(self._items), component=, time=, stage=, **extra) — so the slab type and id/value parity match results.<level>.get(ids=<equiv>). **extra forwards each sub-composite's extra kwargs (gp_indices= for fibers; gp_indices=/layer_indices= for layers); unknown kwargs fail loud in the host's .get.

flow.action: host.get(ids=, component=, time=, stage=, **extra) — re-enters the existing composite read path (node OR element family)
the host's reader.read_* returns the matching frozen Slab dataclass

methods.label: ResultChain.in_box
methods.signature: in_box(lo, hi, *, inclusive: bool = False) -> ResultChain
methods.summary: Refining verb (inherited from SelectionChain): keep atoms whose coordinate row is in the box. Default half-open [lo,hi); inclusive=True → closed [lo,hi]. Coordinates are node coords for node-level chains or fail-loud element centroids for element-level chains (computed/memoised on the engine adapter; an unknown connectivity node id raises). Returns a new ResultChain (covariant, same engine) for further chaining.

flow.action: _coords_of(atoms): node level → fem.nodes.coords via _node_row_map; element level → _centroid_map (fail-loud)
Results._fem supplies nodes.coords/ids + elements.types/resolve for centroids

methods.label: ResultChain.in_sphere
methods.summary: Refining verb (inherited): keep atoms within radius of center (closed ball; ValueError on negative radius). Uses the same per-level coordinate source as in_box. Returns a new ResultChain (covariant, same engine).

methods.label: ResultChain.intersect / &
methods.summary: Set-algebra verb (inherited): keep atoms present in both chains (self order preserved). Same _compatible gate as union (cross-level/results raises). Returns a new ResultChain.

flow.action: _wrap(a for a in self if a in set(other))

methods.inputs: other (a ResultChain from the same composite/level)

methods.label: ResultChain iteration / len / bool / repr
methods.summary: Inherited SelectionChain introspection over the narrowed atoms: iterate the selected node/element ids, len() the count, truthiness = non-empty, repr shows FAMILY='point' and atom count. Lets the user inspect a selection before the terminal .get(component=).

flow.action: expose self._items (the de-duplicated narrowed id tuple)

methods.outputs: iterator / int / bool / str over the selected ids

methods.label: ResultChain.nearest_to
methods.summary: Refining verb (inherited from SelectionChain, point-family default _nearest): keep the count atoms closest to point (squared distance via math.fsum, deterministic lowest-index tie-break). Per-level coordinate source as in_box. Returns a new ResultChain (covariant, same engine).

flow.action: _nearest sorts by squared distance, take first count → _wrap new ResultChain

methods.label: ResultChain.on_plane
methods.summary: Refining verb (inherited): keep atoms within tol of the plane (point + normal; ValueError on zero-length normal or negative tol). Uses the same per-level coordinate source as in_box. Returns a new ResultChain (covariant, same engine).

methods.label: ResultChain.result
methods.summary: Inherited SelectionChain terminal, overridden to fail loud: a results selection identifies node/element ids but a slab read needs a component, so _materialize raises RuntimeError directing the user to .get(component=...) instead of a bare .result().

methods.label: ResultChain.symmetric_difference / ^
methods.summary: Set-algebra verb (inherited): keep atoms in exactly one chain, insertion order preserved across self then other. Same _compatible gate as union. Returns a new ResultChain.
methods.inputs: other (a ResultChain from the same composite/level)

methods.label: ResultChain.union / |
methods.summary: Set-algebra verb (inherited): ordered-dedup concatenation of two ResultChains. _compatible enforces same chain type and same engine identity — two selections from the same results.<level> compose; cross-level / cross-Results / cross-derive raise TypeError. Returns a new ResultChain.

flow.action: _compatible(other): type + engine-identity gate (engine_for memoised per composite/level)

methods.inputs: other (a ResultChain from the same composite/level)

methods.label: ResultChain.where
methods.summary: Refining verb (inherited): keep atoms whose coordinate row satisfies predicate(xyz). Per-level coordinate source as in_box. Returns a new ResultChain (covariant, same engine).

flow.action: filter atoms by predicate(xyz) → _wrap new ResultChain

writes: closes ResultsReader file handle on exit

methods.label: __repr__
methods.summary: Repr of a Results is its inspect.summary() — path, bound-FEM node/element counts + snapshot_id, and the per-stage listing. Lets `results` echo a readable summary in a REPL/notebook.

methods.label: bind
methods.summary: Re-bind to a fresh-session FEMData (carries labels/Parts the embedded snapshot may lack). resolve_bound_fem returns the candidate verbatim with no snapshot_id validation, then ._derive produces a new Results sharing the reader and stages-cache but with the swapped FEM and fresh composites. Pairing a consistent FEMData with the results file is the user's responsibility.

flow.action: resolve_bound_fem(self._reader, fem) returns candidate fem unchanged (no hash check)
self._derive(fem=bound) — clone Results, share reader + _stages_cache, swap _fem, rebuild nodes/elements/inspect composites

methods.outputs: Results (derived, re-bound; same reader/stages, new composites)

methods.label: close
methods.summary: Close the underlying reader and release the HDF5 file handle (no-op if the reader has no close()). Important on Windows where an open handle blocks re-running a capture that recreates the same .h5.

flow.action: if reader has close(): reader.close() releases the HDF5 handle

methods.label: elements
methods.summary: Per-element-node force composite plus the five sub-composites .gauss / .fibers / .layers / .line_stations / .springs (all built in __init__/._derive). Inherits the selection vocabulary and element-centroid geometric selectors.

methods.label: fem
methods.summary: The currently bound FEMData snapshot (the one selectors resolve pg=/label=/selection= against), or None if Results was constructed bare with no embedded/explicit FEM.

methods.label: frequency_hz
methods.summary: Modal natural frequency in Hz for a mode-scoped Results. Raises AttributeError unless the scoped stage has kind=='mode'.

methods.label: from_mpco
methods.signature: classmethod from_mpco(path: str | Path | list[str | Path], *, fem: Optional[FEMData] = None, merge_partitions: bool = True) -> Results
methods.summary: Open a STKO .mpco HDF5 results file. Single path → MPCOReader; a .part-N path (or explicit list) auto-discovers siblings via discover_partition_files and builds an MPCOMultiPartitionReader that stitches boundary nodes (first-occurrence dedup) and concatenates elements across partitions. resolve_bound_fem synthesizes a partial FEMData from the MPCO MODEL/ group when fem= is omitted (no apeGmsh labels / Part provenance). merge_partitions=False reads only the named file.

flow.action: if list/tuple: MPCOMultiPartitionReader(paths) for >1 else MPCOReader(paths[0]); else discover_partition_files(anchor) (unless merge_partitions=False) → multi vs single reader
reader.fem() synthesizes a partial FEMData from MPCO MODEL/ group
resolve_bound_fem(reader, fem) prefers candidate fem= else the synthesized MPCO snapshot
Results(reader, fem=bound_fem, path=anchor) — unscoped, partition-stitched slabs are transparent

methods.inputs: path (one .mpco / .part-N .mpco / list of partition paths), optional fem=, merge_partitions flag

methods.label: from_native
methods.signature: classmethod from_native(path: str | Path, *, fem: Optional[FEMData] = None) -> Results
methods.summary: Open an apeGmsh native HDF5 results file. Constructs a NativeReader over the path, then resolve_bound_fem picks the FEMData: explicit fem= wins, else the reader's embedded /model/ snapshot is reconstructed. No snapshot_id equality is enforced (hash is computed/stored for metadata only); pairing is the user's responsibility. Returns a top-level (unscoped) Results carrying all stages.

flow.action: construct NativeReader(path) over the native HDF5
reader.fem() reconstructs the embedded FEMData snapshot from /model/
resolve_bound_fem(reader, fem) returns candidate fem= if given else the embedded snapshot (no snapshot_id hash check)
Results(reader, fem=bound_fem, path=Path(path)) — builds nodes/elements/inspect composites, _stage_id=None (unscoped)

methods.inputs: path (native .h5 file), optional fem= FEMData override
methods.outputs: Results (unscoped, bound to FEMData, all stages visible)

methods.label: from_recorders
methods.signature: classmethod from_recorders(spec, output_dir: str | Path, *, fem: FEMData, cache_root=None, stage_name='analysis', stage_kind='transient', file_format='out', stage_id=None) -> Results
methods.summary: Open an OpenSees run driven by Tcl/Py recorders. Requires fem= (TypeError otherwise). Builds a cache key from source file mtime+size, PARSER_VERSION and fem.snapshot_id; on cache miss runs RecorderTranscoder to transcode the .out/.xml files at output_dir into a native HDF5 at cache_root, then delegates to from_native(cached_h5, fem=fem). stage_id filters per-stage filename prefixes; Phase 6 v1 supports nodal records only.

flow.action: require fem (else TypeError); if stage_id set and stage_name default, mirror stage_name=stage_id
if cached_h5 missing: RecorderTranscoder(spec, out_dir, cached_h5, fem, ...).run() parses .out/.xml into native HDF5
cls.from_native(cached_h5, fem=fem) — reopen the transcoded HDF5 through NativeReader

methods.inputs: spec (ResolvedRecorderSpec), output_dir (.out/.xml dir), required fem=, cache_root, stage_name/kind/id, file_format
methods.outputs: Results (unscoped; backed by cached native HDF5)
writes: cached native HDF5 at cache_root (compute_cache_key keyed)

methods.label: inspect

methods.label: kind
methods.summary: Stage kind ('transient' | 'static' | 'mode') for a stage-scoped Results. Raises AttributeError on an unscoped instance (use results.stage(...) or results.modes[i] first).

methods.label: mode_index
methods.summary: Mode number for a mode-scoped Results (may be None if the writer didn't tag it). Raises AttributeError unless the scoped stage has kind=='mode'.

methods.label: modes
methods.summary: Stages with kind=='mode' as a list of mode-scoped Results (one ._derive(stage_id=) per modal stage), in write order. Each element exposes .mode_index/.eigenvalue/.frequency_hz/.period_s plus the normal .nodes/.elements scoped reads. Sort by .mode_index for stable indexing.
methods.outputs: list[Results] (each scoped to a kind='mode' stage)

methods.label: n_steps
methods.summary: Number of time steps in a stage-scoped Results' stage (1 for modes). Raises AttributeError when unscoped.

methods.label: name
methods.summary: Stage name for a stage-scoped Results. Raises AttributeError when unscoped.

methods.label: nodes
methods.summary: Node-level result access composite (built in Results.__init__ / ._derive). Mirrors FEMData.nodes selection vocabulary: .get(), .select(), and the geometric selectors nearest_to/in_box/in_sphere/on_plane. All reads resolve the stage via the owning Results and selectors via the bound FEMData.

flow.action: NodeResultsComposite(self) — holds back-ref _r to the owning Results

methods.label: period_s
methods.summary: Modal period in seconds for a mode-scoped Results. Raises AttributeError unless the scoped stage has kind=='mode'.

methods.label: plot
methods.summary: Static matplotlib renderer (lazily constructed, memoised on _plot). Mirrors the interactive viewer's diagram catalog as headless figures — results.plot.contour(...), .deformed(...), .history(...). Requires the [plot] extra.

flow.action: if _plot is None: import + construct ResultsPlot(self), cache on _plot
return the cached ResultsPlot bound to this Results

methods.label: stage
methods.summary: Return a Results scoped to one stage, matched by StageInfo.id or .name (KeyError listing available names/ids on miss). The scoped instance shares the reader/stages-cache via ._derive but carries _stage_id, so subsequent .get() reads resolve to that stage without needing stage=. Scoped instances expose .kind/.name/.n_steps/.time.

flow.action: _lookup_stage(name_or_id) scans _all_stages() for id==name_or_id or name==name_or_id (KeyError on miss)
self._derive(stage_id=info.id) — clone with _stage_id set, fresh composites

methods.label: stages
methods.summary: All stages in the file (also available on scoped instances). Lists StageInfo records (id, name, kind in transient|static|mode, n_steps, plus eigenvalue/frequency_hz/period_s/mode_index for modes). Cached: the reader's stages() is called once and memoised on _stages_cache.

flow.action: _all_stages() lazily calls reader.stages() once, caches on _stages_cache

axis given -> single Selection else dict of 3 Selections

methods.signature: SectionCutDef(plane_point, plane_normal, element_ids, side='positive', label=None, bounding_polygon=None); .from_plane_and_pg(...); .from_planar_pg(...); .preflight(fem,...); .to_spec(); .save_pickle/.load_pickle
methods.summary: The apeGmsh-side frozen, picklable section-cut carrier. Builders from_plane_and_pg / from_planar_pg resolve a physical group to FEM eids via FEMData and map them to OpenSees tags via FemToOpsTagMap(model_h5). preflight() validates against a live FEMData; to_spec() lazily converts to STKO_to_python's SectionCutSpec.

flow.action: user calls ops.h5(path, cuts=, sweeps=); reads fem.snapshot_id; self.build() freezes a BuiltModel; constructs H5Emitter(model_name,snapshot_id)
BuiltModel.emit(H5Emitter) drives every Protocol method; H5Emitter buffers each into typed records (materials/sections/transforms/element_meta/time_series/patterns/recorders/analysis), not a stream

open h5py.File(w): _try_write_broker_zone(fem) writes /meta+neutral zone (or bridge _write_meta fallback for stub FEM), emitter.write_opensees_into(f) appends /opensees/... groups, write_cuts_into(f,cuts,sweeps)

methods.outputs: a dual-zone model.h5 archive at path (/meta + neutral zone + /opensees/...); returns None

flow.action: user calls ops.py(path); self.build() freezes a BuiltModel; constructs PyEmitter
BuiltModel.emit(PyEmitter) drives every Protocol method; PyEmitter renders each as an ops.<method>(...) source line (Tcl block dialect → openseespy implicit current-section/pattern state)
emitter.lines() joined and written to path; if run=True subprocess the resolved python binary on the script

methods.label: from_mpco_model

methods.summary: Reconstruct a FEMData from its embedded /model/ group (nodes, per-type elements, PGs, labels, mesh_selection); loads/masses/constraints are not round-tripped.

flow.action: read /model/nodes + /model/elements + named groups, build composites; snapshot_id matches the embedded one

methods.outputs: FEMData with empty BC composites; snapshot_id parity for Results.bind()

methods.label: snapshot_id

flow.action: _hash_nodes (argsort node ids, tobytes coords) + _hash_elements (type-name order, argsort eids+conn) + _hash_physical_groups (sorted (dim,tag), sorted member nids) → blake2b.hexdigest()

methods.label: to_h5
methods.signature: to_h5(path: str, *, model_name: str = '', apegmsh_version: str = '', ndf: int = 0) -> None
methods.summary: Write a fresh root-level model.h5 containing only the neutral zone (no /opensees) — everything the broker knows about the model.

flow.action: delegates to write_fem_h5(self, path, model_name, apegmsh_version, ndf)
h5py.File('w'); write_meta stamps schema_version 2.4.0 + snapshot_id; write_neutral_zone writes 8 groups

methods.outputs: model.h5 file on disk (neutral zone only)

methods.label: to_native_h5

methods.label: hrz_weights
methods.summary: from apeGmsh.fem import _hrz — normalized per-node HRZ (Hinton-Rock-Zienkiewicz) lumping weights = diag(∫N_I²) / Σ on the reference element, summing to 1.0 (1/n for first-order). lru_cached. Used by the mass resolver's consistent-reduction path.

flow.action: look up _QUAD_SPEC + shape fn, evaluate N at quadrature pts

methods.outputs: tuple of per-node weights summing to 1.0

methods.label: gauss_hex_3d
methods.summary: n×n×n tensor-product Gauss-Legendre on [-1,+1]^3; weights sum to 8. Reference hex domain matching hex8/20/27 shape functions.

methods.label: gauss_legendre_1d
methods.summary: from apeGmsh.fem import _quadrature — n-point Gauss-Legendre on [-1,+1] (np.polynomial.legendre.leggauss), exact to degree 2n-1. Returns (points (n,), weights (n,)) matching the line shape-function parent domain.

methods.label: gauss_quad_2d
methods.summary: n×n tensor-product Gauss-Legendre on [-1,+1]^2; weights sum to 4. Reference quad domain matching quad4/8/9 shape functions.

methods.label: gauss_tet
methods.summary: 11-point Keast rule on the reference tet (origin + 3 unit-axis vertices), degree-4 exact; weights normalized to volume 1/6.

methods.label: gauss_tri
methods.summary: 7-point Strang-Fix/Hammer rule on the reference triangle (0,0)/(1,0)/(0,1), degree-5 exact; weights sum to area 1/2.

methods.label: gauss_wedge
methods.summary: Tensor product of the 7-point triangle rule and an n_line-point 1-D line rule for the reference wedge; weights sum to 1.
methods.inputs: n_line points along the prism axis

methods.label: compute_jacobian_dets
methods.summary: Per-element per-IP integration measures: |det J| for 'solid', ‖∂x/∂ξ×∂x/∂η‖ for 'shell', ‖∂x/∂ξ‖ for 'line'. Always non-negative; raises on unknown geom_kind.

flow.action: dN_fn(nat) → einsum Jacobian, det/cross/norm by geom_kind

methods.label: compute_physical_coords
methods.summary: Vectorized isoparametric map: einsum('in,enj->eij', N(nat), node_coords) → physical (x,y,z) at every IP for every element. Batched over elements sharing an IP layout.

flow.action: evaluate N_fn(natural_coords), einsum with node coords

methods.label: shape-fn primitives
methods.summary: Per-element-type shape-function (N: (n_ip,n_nodes)) and derivative (dN: (n_ip,n_nodes,parent_dim)) primitives, exported so users can plug them into custom catalog keys. Natural-coord conventions match Gmsh exactly.

flow.action: evaluate basis/derivatives at natural coords (pure numpy)

methods.label: get_shape_functions
methods.signature: get_shape_functions(gmsh_code: int) -> Optional[Tuple[ShapeFn, ShapeFn, GeomKind, int]]
methods.summary: from apeGmsh.fem import _shape_functions — dispatch by Gmsh element-type code to (N_fn, dN_fn, geom_kind, n_nodes); covers line2..hex27. Returns None for uncatalogued codes. Read by the mass resolver / FEM extract side.

methods.label: bc
methods.signature: bc(target=None, *, pg=None, label=None, tag=None, dofs=None, name=None) -> BCDef
methods.summary: Declares a homogeneous single-point constraint (fix-to-ground) on a dimension-agnostic target; stored apart from constraint_defs and resolved to fem.nodes.sp, not fem.nodes.constraints.

flow.action: coalesce target via loads._coalesce_target, build BCDef, append to self._bc_defs (pre-mesh)
BCDef held until get_fem_data() triggers resolve_bcs
resolve_bcs(): loads._target_nodes resolves target->mesh nodes (label->PG->tag->mesh-selection, any dim), fail-loud on zero nodes; emit one SPRecord per masked DOF per node
lands on fem.nodes.sp (same channel as g.loads.face_sp)

methods.inputs: target (str|list[(dim,tag)]) or pg=/label=/tag=; dofs restraint mask [ux,uy,uz,rx,ry,rz] default [1,1,1]; name
methods.outputs: BCDef (also appended to self._bc_defs); resolves to homogeneous SPRecords on fem.nodes.sp

methods.label: clear
methods.summary: Clears all stored constraint defs and resolved records (does not clear _bc_defs).

methods.label: distributing_coupling
methods.signature: distributing_coupling(master_label, slave_label, *, master_point=(0.,0.,0.), dofs=None, weighting="uniform", name=None) -> DistributingCouplingDef [raises NotImplementedError]
methods.summary: Not implemented — always raises NotImplementedError (the prior RBE3 implementation silently emitted a mechanically wrong kinematic mean); parameters accepted only so the error message is actionable.

flow.action: raise NotImplementedError immediately (no def stored, no resolution); defence-in-depth twin in ConstraintResolver.resolve_distributing

methods.inputs: master_label, slave_label, master_point, dofs, weighting, name (all accepted but unused)

methods.label: embedded
methods.signature: embedded(host_label, embedded_label, *, tolerance=1.0, host_entities=None, embedded_entities=None, name=None) -> EmbeddedDef

flow.action: for each LoadDef with str target, call _resolve_target -> core._resolution.resolve_target (raises on miss)

methods.label: line
methods.signature: line(target=None, *, pg=None, label=None, tag=None, linear_density: float, rotational=None, dofs=None, reduction="lumped", name=None) -> LineMassDef
methods.summary: Declares a distributed line mass (mass per unit length) on curve(s); resolver distributes by tributary length (lumped) or consistent line2 matrix and accumulates into the MassSet on fem.nodes.masses.

flow.action: _resolve_line_lumped/_consistent: _target_edges resolves curve edges, MassResolver.resolve_line_lumped (½ρ_lL each) or resolve_line_consistent; resolve() accumulates per node
per-node accumulation flattened, wrapped in MassSet

methods.inputs: target or pg=/label=/tag=; linear_density (required); rotational; dofs subset {1,2,3}; reduction lumped|consistent; name
methods.outputs: LineMassDef; resolves to accumulated MassRecord on fem.nodes.masses

methods.label: point
methods.signature: point(target=None, *, pg=None, label=None, tag=None, mass: float, rotational=None, dofs=None, reduction="lumped", name=None) -> PointMassDef
methods.summary: Declares a concentrated mass (+ optional rotational inertia) at every node of a target; resolver applies the same length-6 mass vector to each node and accumulates into the MassSet on fem.nodes.masses.

flow.action: _coalesce_target, validate dofs/rotational, _add_def checks reduction in _DISPATCH, append PointMassDef (pre-mesh)
_resolve_point: _target_nodes resolves target->node set, MassResolver.resolve_point_lumped builds _build_vec6 per node; resolve() accumulates per-node across all defs
per-node accumulation flattened to one MassRecord/node, wrapped in MassSet

methods.inputs: target or pg=/label=/tag=; mass (float, required); rotational (Ixx,Iyy,Izz); dofs translational mask subset {1,2,3}; reduction lumped|consistent; name
methods.outputs: PointMassDef; resolves to accumulated MassRecord on fem.nodes.masses

methods.label: resolve
methods.signature: resolve(node_tags, node_coords, elem_tags=None, connectivity=None, *, node_map=None, face_map=None, ndf=6) -> MassSet
methods.summary: Stage-2 driver auto-called by get_fem_data(): instantiates MassResolver, walks mass_defs via the (type,reduction) _DISPATCH table, accumulates per-node length-6 mass vectors across overlapping targets, and returns a MassSet of one MassRecord per node.

flow.action: construct MassResolver(node_tags,node_coords,elem_tags,connectivity,ndf); per def gather mesh data (_target_nodes/edges/faces/elements) and call MassResolver.resolve_*; sum vectors into per-node accumulator
flow.passes: mesh data + MassDef -> raw MassRecord list summed by node_id

flow.action: build one MassRecord per node from accumulator, store self.mass_records, wrap in MassSet(records)

methods.inputs: node_tags, node_coords, elem_tags, connectivity; node_map, face_map; ndf (default 6)
methods.outputs: MassSet (also stored on self.mass_records); lands on fem.nodes.masses

methods.label: surface
methods.signature: surface(target=None, *, pg=None, label=None, tag=None, areal_density: float, rotational=None, derive_rotational=False, dofs=None, reduction="lumped", name=None) -> SurfaceMassDef
methods.summary: Declares a distributed surface mass (mass per unit area) on face(s); resolver distributes ρ_a·A per face by tributary/HRZ (optionally shape-function-derived rotational inertia) and accumulates into the MassSet on fem.nodes.masses.

flow.action: _coalesce_target, validate dofs/rotational/derive_rotational (consistent-only, mutually exclusive with rotational), _add_def append SurfaceMassDef (pre-mesh)

_resolve_surface_lumped/_consistent: _target_faces resolves face conn, MassResolver.resolve_surface_lumped (ρ_a·A/n) or resolve_surface_consistent (HRZ / _hrz_distribute_derived); resolve() accumulates
per-node accumulation flattened, wrapped in MassSet

methods.inputs: target or pg=/label=/tag=; areal_density (required); rotational; derive_rotational (consistent-only); dofs subset {1,2,3}; reduction; name
methods.outputs: SurfaceMassDef; resolves to accumulated MassRecord on fem.nodes.masses

methods.label: validate_pre_mesh
methods.summary: Eagerly resolves every string mass target via _resolve_target so typos fail fast before meshing; raw (dim,tag) lists are skipped.

methods.label: volume
methods.signature: volume(target=None, *, pg=None, label=None, tag=None, density: float, rotational=None, derive_rotational=False, dofs=None, reduction="lumped", name=None) -> VolumeMassDef
methods.summary: Declares distributed mass from material density over volume(s); resolver distributes ρ·V per element by tributary/HRZ (optionally shape-function-derived parallel-axis rotational inertia) and accumulates into the MassSet on fem.nodes.masses.

flow.action: _resolve_volume_lumped/_consistent: _target_elements resolves volume conn, MassResolver.resolve_volume_lumped (ρ·V/n) or resolve_volume_consistent (HRZ via element_volume isoparametric); resolve() accumulates
per-node accumulation flattened, wrapped in MassSet

methods.label: set
methods.summary: Declares the per-node DOF count (ndf) for every node resolved from target (label / PG / part / mesh-selection / DimTag list); resolved at FEM-build time by _fem_factory._populate_node_ndf and written into NodeComposite._ndf. Explicit-only — apeGmsh does NOT infer ndf from element class (ADR 0032). Validates ndf in [1, 6]; multiple set calls may overlap (last-matching-def-wins).

flow.action: held until get_fem_data() -> _fem_factory._populate_node_ndf walks defs in declaration order

_populate_node_ndf resolves each def's target via core._resolution.resolve_target, writes ndf into NodeComposite._ndf int8 array; orphan-filtered tags logged as cohort warning; KeyError on unresolvable target propagates per the dimensional resolution contract
fem.nodes.ndf_for(nid) returns declared value; sentinel 0 raises LookupError naming both fixes (set + set_default)

flow.action: _default_def() returns the default; _fem_factory._populate_node_ndf fills every node still at the sentinel after all targeted defs resolve

methods.label: DomainCapture
methods.signature: DomainCapture(spec: ResolvedDomainCaptureSpec, path, fem: FEMData, *, ops=None)
methods.summary: Context manager that wraps a ResolvedDomainCaptureSpec + target HDF5 path and records an in-process openseespy analysis (user drives the loop, calls step(t)). ndm/ndf ride on the resolved spec. Usually constructed via ops.domain_capture(...) or DomainCapture.from_h5(...).

flow.action: store resolved spec, output Path, fem, ndm/ndf from spec; defer NativeWriter to __enter__

methods.inputs: ResolvedDomainCaptureSpec, output path, FEMData, optional ops module

methods.label: begin_stage
methods.summary: Open a new capture stage, reset all per-record buffers; the time vector and datasets are written lazily at end_stage (step count not known up-front). Returns the stage name.

flow.action: validate writer open + no stage already open; reset every category capturer's per-stage buffers

methods.label: capture_modes
methods.summary: Run ops.eigen(n_modes) and write one kind='mode' single-step stage per mode (eigenvalue/frequency/period metadata + nodeEigenvector translational/rotational shapes). n_modes defaults to the max across the spec's modal records.

flow.action: determine n_modes from modal records; lazily resolve ops and call ops.eigen(n_modes)
ops.eigen returns eigenvalues; ops.nodeEigenvector queried per node/mode/axis
per mode NativeWriter.begin_stage(kind='mode', eigenvalue/freq/period) + write_nodes(displacement_*/rotation_*) + end_stage
one mode stage written per eigenpair

methods.inputs: optional n_modes override; live openseespy domain
methods.outputs: None (one mode-kind stage per mode written)

methods.label: close
methods.summary: Finalise any open stage (auto end_stage) and close the underlying NativeWriter; also invoked by __exit__.

flow.action: if a stage is open call end_stage(), then NativeWriter.close()

methods.label: end_stage
methods.summary: Flush all buffered stage data to disk: derive the shared time vector, NativeWriter.begin_stage, merge per-record node buffers (union ids, NaN-fill), write gauss/line/nodal/fiber/layer groups, close the stage; emits a consolidated skipped-element warning.

flow.action: pick the stage time vector from the first non-empty capturer
merge node buffers to one (T,Nunion) slab; call writer.write_nodes / write_gauss_group / write_line_stations_group / write_nodal_forces_group / write_fibers_group / write_layers_group
datasets written into the stage group; writer.end_stage closes it

methods.outputs: None (stage written into the native HDF5)

methods.label: from_h5
methods.signature: DomainCapture.from_h5(model_path, *, spec: DomainCaptureSpec, fem: FEMData, output, ops=None) -> DomainCapture
methods.summary: Construct a DomainCapture for the non-bridge path: reads ndm/ndf from a bridge-emitted model.h5 /meta, resolves a standalone spec against fem with those dims, returns a ready context manager writing to output.

flow.action: open model.h5 via opensees.emitter.h5_reader, read /meta ndm+ndf
h5_reader returns meta dict with ndm/ndf
return ResolvedDomainCaptureSpec; wrap in a new DomainCapture(resolved, output, fem)

methods.inputs: model.h5 path, standalone DomainCaptureSpec, fem, output path, optional ops

methods.label: step
methods.summary: Capture one snapshot at simulation time t — queries the live openseespy domain (ops.nodeDisp/nodeReaction/eleResponse/...) per category capturer and buffers values in RAM. Raises NotImplementedError for unsupported element-level categories.

flow.action: lazily resolve ops module; if any record needs reactions call ops.reactions()
append per-component (N,) / per-class flat frames to in-RAM buffers

methods.label: DomainCaptureSpec
methods.summary: Declarative spec builder for in-process DomainCapture. Standalone supports declaration+introspection; an attached OpenSees bridge (opensees=ops) is required for .resolve() so ndm/ndf are unambiguous (Phase 9 D8). Public entry of the capture/spec builder API.

flow.action: construct an empty record list; retain optional opensees bridge handle for resolve-time ndm/ndf + class hints

methods.summary: Read component at every node within radius of center (closed ball; ValueError on negative radius). Named selectors restrict candidates first; _node_ids_in_sphere intersects; read via self.get(ids=narrowed,...).

flow.action: _node_ids_in_sphere(fem, center, radius) on fem.nodes.coords; intersect1d with candidate

methods.inputs: center, radius, component (required), optional selectors, time, stage

methods.signature: nearest_to(point, *, component: str, pg=None, label=None, selection=None, ids=None, time=None, stage=None) -> NodeSlab
methods.summary: Read component at the single node nearest point (3D Euclidean). Named selectors first restrict the candidate set (additive), then _nearest_node_id picks the closest from fem.nodes.coords; result is read via self.get(ids=[nid], ...). Requires a bound FEMData.

methods.label: nodes.on_plane

Named selectors restrict candidates first; _node_ids_on_plane intersects; read via self.get(ids=narrowed,...). Useful for story-level / mid-span cuts.

flow.action: _node_ids_on_plane(fem, point, normal, tol) on fem.nodes.coords; intersect1d with candidate

methods.summary: Start a daisy-chainable node-result selection (point family, node level). Returns a ResultChain bound to a stable per-composite engine adapter; refine with in_box/in_sphere/on_plane/nearest_to/where + set algebra, then terminal .get(component=).

methods.label: RecorderTranscoder
methods.signature: RecorderTranscoder(spec, output_dir, target_path, fem, *, stage_name='analysis', stage_kind='transient', file_format='out', stage_id=None)
methods.summary: Public transcoder entry used by Results.from_recorders — parses emitted OpenSees .out files into a native HDF5 file matching the apeGmsh schema, reusing the spec emitter to recompute file paths/column layouts.

flow.action: store spec, output_dir, target_path, fem, stage metadata; defer parsing to run()

methods.label: run
methods.summary: Parse all emitted recorder files and write the native HDF5 target. Per record: re-derive file paths via emit_logical, parse .out via _txt, unflatten element-level data via the response catalog, merge nodes (union+NaN), then NativeWriter writes one stage. fibers/layers/modal skipped (surfaced in self.unsupported).

flow.action: native HDF5 target written and closed

methods.inputs: none (consumes constructor state + on-disk .out files + bound FEMData)
methods.outputs: Path of the written native HDF5 file

methods.label: viewer
methods.signature: Results.viewer(*, blocking=True, title=None, restore_session='prompt', save_session=True, cuts=None, model_h5=None) -> ResultsViewer | subprocess.Popen | None
methods.summary: Public ResultsViewer open entry. Blocking: constructs ResultsViewer(self, ...).show() in-process and runs the Qt loop until close. Non-blocking: spawns `python -m apeGmsh.viewers <path>` and closes the parent reader. Returns None under APEGMSH_SKIP_VIEWER.

flow.action: if APEGMSH_SKIP_VIEWER return None; if not blocking spawn subprocess and self.close()
blocking: construct ResultsViewer(self, title, restore_session, save_session, cuts, model_h5).show()
ResultsViewer.show() pins a live ref, logs session.open, calls _show_impl

methods.inputs: blocking/title/session/cuts/model_h5 options; Results with a bound FEMData

methods.label: NativeWriter
methods.summary: Public bulk writer for apeGmsh native HDF5 result files. Used internally by RecorderTranscoder and DomainCapture: open() writes root attrs + embeds FEMData, begin_stage/end_stage scope stages, write_nodes/write_*_group write per-level slabs at SCHEMA_VERSION.

flow.action: open() creates the h5py.File, stamps SCHEMA_VERSION/source attrs, write_model(fem) embeds the /model snapshot
datasets persisted; close() finalises the file

methods.inputs: output path; then fem + per-stage slab arrays via the write methods
methods.outputs: native HDF5 results file on disk
